meta.title: The Specific Reduction of a and d Antigenic Determinants after Periodate Treatment of Hepatitis B Surface Antigen (HBsAg)

abstractHtml: HBsAg antigenicity was found to be sensitive to periodate treatment. Antigenic determinants a and d were especially sensitive, losing almost all of their activity, while determinants r and w were found to be quite stable. Also, it is now possible to prepare the monospecific antibodies, anti-r and anti-w, by the use of this new, simpler procedure.
